prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response:
# A CPU Explained by Grandma

Oh sweetie, well, you know how I have my brain that helps me remember recipes and tell stories? Well, computers have something like that too, and they call it a CPU - that's the "central processing unit," dear. It's like the heart of the computer, always working away, making decisions and doing all the little calculations so your pictures and games can work properly. I don't quite understand all the technical mumbo-jumbo myself, but I know it's very important and works ever so fast - much faster than this old brain of mine! The faster the CPU is, the quicker your computer can do things, kind of like how I used to work faster in the kitchen when I was younger. It's probably buried somewhere deep inside your computer where you can't see it, just like our brains are tucked inside our heads!
